description: Eval cases for data integrity: PII, migrations, serialization, schema validation

cases.name: Unencrypted PII in database
cases.description: Sensitive personal data stored without encryption
cases.code_snippet:
CREATE TABLE users (
    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    email VARCHAR(255),
    phone VARCHAR(20),
    ssn VARCHAR(11),
    credit_card VARCHAR(20),
    date_of_birth DATE
);

def save_user(email, phone, ssn, cc):
    query = '''INSERT INTO users (email, phone, ssn, credit_card)
              VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s)'''
    db.execute(query, (email, phone, ssn, cc))

cases.name: Unsafe database migration
cases.description: Migration that could cause data loss
cases.code_snippet:
def migrate():
    # Drops column without backup
    db.execute('ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN legacy_field')
    # No DEFAULT value - NULLs all existing records
    db.execute('ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN status VARCHAR(50)')
    # No index on frequently queried column
    db.execute('ALTER TABLE orders ADD COLUMN customer_id INT')

cases.name: Unsafe deserialization
Missing schema validation
cases.description: Storing data without validating against schema
cases.code_snippet:
def save_product(data):
    # No schema validation
    product = Product()
    product.name = data.get('name')
    product.price = data.get('price')  # Could be string, negative, null
    product.description = data.get('description')  # No length check
    product.tags = data.get('tags')  # Could be any type
    db.save(product)
    return product

cases.name: Denormalized data without synchronization
cases.description: Redundant data stored without updating synchronously
cases.code_snippet:
def update_user_email(user_id, new_email):
    # Updates primary table
    db.execute('UPDATE users SET email = %s WHERE id = %s', (new_email, user_id))
    # Denormalized copy in cache - could get out of sync
    # No cache invalidation
    return True

cases.name: Properly validated and encrypted data storage
cases.description: Data with encryption, validation, and schema enforcement
